Cross-Retailer Assortment Intelligence

Women’s Jeans Comparison

Comparing assortment pricing, promotional activity, and product attributes across nine retailer groups: Target (Owned & National), Walmart Owned Brands, Amazon Owned Brands, American Eagle, Old Navy, Macy’s, Kohl’s, and Levi’s.

Unit of Analysis: Color Combination (CC)
Each row represents one product × color combination. A single jean style available in 5 colors = 5 CCs. All counts, distributions, and n= values are measured in CCs. Prices reflect per-unit pricing (Walmart multi-packs are divided to single-unit price).
Data Collection & Methodology
Data collected: April 15–16, 2026 via automated product page scraping across all nine retailers.

What we captured: Every women’s jeans product page was visited and we recorded the full set of available colors, current and original pricing, product attributes (rise, leg shape, length, fit), fabric composition, and promotional status. Each color option on a product page becomes one row in our dataset.

Standardization: Raw data from each retailer uses different naming conventions, so we normalized all attributes into a common format. Color names (e.g., “Erasmus,” “Moon Stone Light”) were mapped into wash categories (Light, Medium, Dark, Black, etc.) using keyword matching. Rise, leg shape, fit, and length descriptions were similarly standardized. For Walmart multi-packs, we calculated per-unit pricing so items are comparable across retailers.

Coverage: Target — 2,673 CCs across 58 brands (3 owned brands: Universal Thread, Wild Fable, Ava & Viv, plus 55 national brands). Walmart — 406 CCs across 4 owned brands (Free Assembly, No Boundaries, Time and Tru, Terra & Sky). Old Navy — 289 CCs across 70 product pages. American Eagle — 170 CCs across 86 products. Amazon — 78 CCs across 2 owned brands (Amazon Essentials, The Drop). Macy’s — 335 CCs across product pages. Kohl’s — 520 CCs across product pages. Levi’s — 636 CCs across product pages.
00

Assortment Summary

Products & Color Combos
Avg Colors per Product
Median Price: Current vs Original
Promotional Activity
01

Price Architecture — Summary

How to Read a Box Plot
Each bar visualizes the full price distribution for a retailer or brand. Here is what each element means:

The colored box spans from Q1 (25th percentile) to Q3 (75th percentile) — the middle 50% of all prices fall inside this range. A wider box means more price spread.

The vertical white line inside the box marks the median (50th percentile) — half of items cost less, half cost more.

The ◆ diamond marks the mean (average price). When the mean is higher than the median, a few expensive items are pulling the average up.

The thin horizontal lines (whiskers) extend from the box to show the range of typical prices. They stop at Q1−1.5×IQR on the left and Q3+1.5×IQR on the right (where IQR = Q3−Q1). Items priced beyond the whiskers are statistical outliers and are not shown, keeping the chart readable.

The numbers above the whisker caps show the whisker-fence values (not the actual min/max). Hover or tap a bar to see the full stats including actual min, max, and sample size in the tooltip.

Walmart prices use per-unit price (total ÷ pack size) so 2-packs are comparable to singles.
Price Distribution by Retailer Group
Box = IQR (Q1–Q3), white line = median, ◆ = mean, caps = whisker fence. Min/max labeled. Click a group to drill down.
02

Brand Drill-Down

Select a retailer group to see individual brand price distributions. Other groups appear at bottom as reference bars (lighter) for comparison. Respects the Current/Original toggle.

Select a group to see its brands. Other groups docked at bottom for reference.

Select a group above
03

Promotional Activity & Discount Depth

On Sale = CC where current price < original price. Discount % = (original − current) ÷ original × 100. Distribution shows relative % of on-sale CCs per discount bucket (normalizes for sample size).
% of Assortment on Sale
Share of CCs where current price < original price.
Share of CCs currently discounted
Avg Discount Depth
Mean discount % among CCs currently on sale only. Shows how deep each retailer cuts.
Mean discount % among on-sale CCs
Discount Distribution
Histogram as % of each group’s on-sale CCs (not absolute count), making different sample sizes comparable.
Relative % of on-sale CCs by discount bucket
04

Attribute Comparisons

Each chart shows % mix within each retailer group. Groups only shown if they have data. n= = CCs with a value.

Stacked 100% bars. Only groups with data for each attribute are included.

Rise
Rise normalized: Low, Mid (includes classic/regular), High, Super High (ultra-high). Parsed from title or product attributes.
Leg Shape
Parsed from title/fit field. Skinny, Slim, Straight, Bootcut, Flare, Wide Leg, Baggy, Barrel, Boyfriend, Relaxed, Jegging, Crop, Tapered.
Fit Style
Fit Style Taxonomy — Derived from leg shape:
Slim/Contemporary: Skinny, Slim, Jegging
Regular: Straight, Bootcut, Flare, Barrel, Tapered, Mom, Trouser
Relaxed: Wide Leg, Baggy, Boyfriend, Relaxed, Loose
Curvy: Curvy-designated styles
Fabric Weight
Cotton Content
Fabric Weight
Cotton Content
Wash / Color Mix
Color names mapped to wash buckets: Light Wash, Medium Wash, Dark Wash, Black, White/Cream, Grey, Brown/Earth, Green, Pink/Red, Yellow/Orange, Purple, Other.
05

Color & Wash — Sunburst Deep Dive

Inner ring = wash category. Outer ring = individual color names. Hover for CC count and %.

Inner ring = wash category, outer ring = individual colors. Hover for details.

Heatmap Explorer

Cross-tab of two attribute dimensions. Each cell shows % of CCs in that combination for each group. Cell shading indicates which group over-indexes (has a higher % share) in that attribute intersection. Stronger color = larger gap.

Cross-tab two attribute dimensions and compare how two retailer groups distribute across combinations.

Strategic Analysis

Key Insights

Data-driven findings from the cross-retailer assortment analysis. Each insight is calculated from the full dataset.

01
Style Selection Too Narrow
Target Owned Brands offer only 43 unique jean styles compared to Target National Brands at 892 styles and Levi's at 179 styles. This limits our ability to capture customer consideration across different silhouettes.
02
Too Few Color/Wash Variations
Target OB averages 2.2 colors per style vs the cross-retailer average of 3.2. Limited wash options reduce shelf impact and restrict customer choice.
03
Tightest Pricing Band
Target OB pricing spans just $26 (from $14 to $40), the narrowest range of any retailer studied. Even premium brands like Levi’s maintain a $400 range, and American Eagle spans $119. There are no graduation tiers for customers to trade up.
04
OB Brands Compete Against Themselves
After discounting, 98% of Target OB CCs overlap in price with another Target OB brand. Universal Thread at current price ($28.00 median) overlaps directly with Wild Fable ($32.00 median).
05
OB Brands Overlap with National Brands
At original pricing, Target OB overlaps with 19% of NB price points. At current (discounted) price, overlap increases to 46%. Discounting erodes planned differentiation.
06
Ceding Entry-Level
Target OB median current price is $30.00 vs Amazon OB at $19.40 (35% lower) and Walmart OB at $19.98 (33% lower). Price-conscious customers have clear lower-cost alternatives.
07
Over-Exposed on Low Rise
23% of Target OB CCs are Low Rise vs market average of 5%. This exposes Target to trend risk if the low-rise cycle shifts.
08
Wash Mix Assessment
Target OB indexes at 23% Light Wash vs market average of 10%. This over-indexes vs market and limits appeal to customers preferring medium and dark washes.
Strategic Analysis

Key Insights

Data-driven findings from the cross-retailer assortment analysis. Each insight is calculated from the full dataset.

01

Target Owned Brands offer only 43 unique jean styles compared to Target National Brands at 892 styles and Levi's at 179 styles. This limits our ability to capture customer consideration across different silhouettes.

Target OB averages 2.2 colors per style vs the cross-retailer average of 4.2. Limited wash options reduce shelf impact and restrict customer choice.

Target OB pricing spans just $26 (from $14 to $40), the narrowest range of any retailer studied. Even premium brands like Levi’s maintain a $400 range, and American Eagle spans $119. There are no graduation tiers for customers to trade up.

Cross-Retailer Women’s Jeans Dashboard — 9 Retailer Groups — April 2026
